chunks.content: Document 2 chunk 0: Machine learning models require large datasets for effective training.
Document 2 chunk 1: Neural networks consist of interconnected layers of artificial neurons.
Document 2 chunk 2: Natural language processing enables computers to understand human text.
Document 2 chunk 3: Computer vision algorithms detect and classify objects in images.
Document 2 chunk 4: Reinforcement learning agents learn through interaction with environments.
Document 2 chunk 5: Transfer learning reuses pre-trained models for new tasks.
Document 2 chunk 6: Feature engineering transforms raw data into meaningful representations.
Document 2 chunk 7: Model evaluation metrics measure prediction accuracy and reliability.
Document 2 chunk 8: Gradient descent optimizes model parameters to minimize loss functions.
Document 2 chunk 9: Data preprocessing handles missing values and normalizes features.
